content.problemStatement: The chef got a new rabbit and he is going to train him so that he can perform for him whenever he needs entertainment. The chef teaches k types of jumps to the rabbit. Each jump has definite length L units. The rabbit does not have any brains he will use any type of jump he feels like at any point of time. He is placed on a very long mat and starts at 0 unit. The chef wants to know in how many ways he can perform his jumps and cover exactly N units of distance.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: a line with a single integer N, the required total distance, and a line starting with an integer k followed by k distinct integers L_i, the lengths of the jumps the rabbit can use.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the number of different ordered sequences of jumps that make the rabbit travel exactly N units, modulo 1000000007.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case, the number of ways to reach the distance N is calculated using dynamic programming.
The rabbit can reach the distance 5 in 3 ways: 1+1+1+1+1, 1+1+1+2, 1+2+2.

solution.approach: This problem can be solved using dynamic programming. The idea is to maintain a DP array where dp[i] represents the number of ways to reach the distance i. For each jump length L_i, we update dp[i] by adding the number of ways to reach the distance i - L_i. We use modular arithmetic to avoid overflow.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N * k) per test case, O(T * N * k) total. Precomputation is not required.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — the dominant structure is the DP array.

code.Python3:
import sys

MOD = 1000000007

T = int(sys.stdin.readline())
while T > 0:
    N = int(sys.stdin.readline())
    k, *L = map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split())
    dp = [0] * (N + 1)
    dp[0] = 1
    for i in range(1, N + 1):
        for j in range(k):
            if i >= L[j]:
                dp[i] = (dp[i] + dp[i - L[j]]) % MOD
    print(dp[N])
    T -= 1
